description: anvilml-ipc: fix ipc-probe binary to use write_frame/read_frame correctly
context: ipc-probe.rs currently hand-rolls a raw WorkerEvent::Pong frame using rmp_serde::to_vec_named(&event) directly, bypassing write_frame. This produces serde native enum encoding, not the flat dict format (_type key) that read_frame expects, causing the _type field missing error. Fix: replace the hand-rolled write side with write_frame(&mut tx, &WorkerMessage::Ping { seq: 7 }).await? (write_frame calls serialize_message which produces the correct flat dict). Remove the manual payload/header construction and the WorkerEvent import (no longer needed on the write side). The read side (read_frame) is already correct and unchanged. Expected output after fix: cargo run -p anvilml-ipc --bin ipc-probe prints OK seq=7 and exits 0 on both Linux and Windows. No changes to framing.rs, messages.rs, or any other file. cargo test -p anvilml-ipc exits 0.

description: anvilml-worker: fix respawn loop — WorkerPool.workers not updated after respawn
context: pool.rs has two separate worker lists: WorkerPool.workers (plain Vec, used by list/acquire_idle/set_busy/set_idle) and shared_workers (Arc<RwLock<Vec>>, updated on respawn). After respawn the public methods still reference the dead worker. Fix: change WorkerPool.workers field from Vec<Arc<ManagedWorker>> to Arc<RwLock<Vec<Arc<ManagedWorker>>>> (same Arc as shared_workers). In spawn_all: construct shared_workers first, store the same Arc in both WorkerPool.workers and the event listener closure (no clone needed — they are the same Arc). Update list(), acquire_idle(), set_busy(), set_idle() to read-lock self.workers. Second bug: after respawn the per-worker event listener task is still subscribed to the dead worker broadcast channel. In the respawn task, after locked[idx] = new_worker: spawn a new per-worker listener task for new_worker using the same pattern as spawn_all (subscribe, loop recv, process Ready/Dead/forward events). The old listener task will break on RecvError::Closed when the dead worker channel closes naturally. No changes to managed.rs, framing.rs, or any other crate. cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware exits 0. Runnable proof: cargo run --features mock-hardware; kill the worker pid; observe /v1/workers shows worker-0 returns to Idle within ~5s on the next poll.

description: anvilml-worker: replace serial_test env-var workaround with scoped env isolation
context: In managed.rs: the four spawning tests (spawn_ping_pong, status_transitions, handshake_completes_once, spawn_reaches_idle) use std::env::set_var("ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK","1") and rely on #[serial_test::serial] to prevent parallel env-var contamination. Replace set_var/remove_var calls in each test body with a temp_env::with_var("ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK",Some("1"), async { ... }) scope (add temp_env to [dev-dependencies]). Remove all four #[serial_test::serial] attributes. Remove serial_test from [dev-dependencies] in anvilml-worker/Cargo.toml. No logic changes to test assertions. cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware exits 0 with all 16 tests passing without ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK set in the ambient shell environment (verify with env -i HOME=$HOME PATH=$PATH cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware).

description: anvilml-worker: retrofit mandatory IPC DEBUG log points (managed.rs)
context: In managed.rs: add tracing::debug!(worker_id = %worker_id, message_type = msg_discriminant(&msg)) in the writer task immediately before each IPC frame is written to stdin. Add tracing::debug!(worker_id = %worker_id, event_type = event_discriminant(&event)) in the reader task immediately after each WorkerEvent is successfully deserialized. msg_discriminant() and event_discriminant() helper fns already exist in this file. These are §11.5 mandatory DEBUG points. No logic changes. cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-worker: retrofit mandatory spawn and status-transition DEBUG log points (pool.rs)
context: In pool.rs: add tracing::debug!(worker_id = %wid, device_index = device_index) immediately after each ManagedWorker::new() call in spawn_all() (covers both GPU and CPU worker creation paths). Add tracing::debug!(worker_id = %worker_id, from = %old_status, to = %new_status) in set_busy() and set_idle() before the status transition. These are §11.5 mandatory DEBUG points. set_busy and set_idle currently log at INFO — keep those, the DEBUG calls are additions. No logic changes. cargo test -p anvilml-worker --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-scheduler: retrofit mandatory job dispatch and state-transition DEBUG log points (scheduler.rs)
context: In scheduler.rs: the submit() method has tracing::info!(job_id = %job_id) but is missing the §11.5 mandatory debug points. Add tracing::debug!(job_id = %job_id, status = "Queued", "job status transition") after insert_job succeeds. The dispatch loop (Phase 13, not yet written) will add job_dispatched and Running transition points — do not anticipate those here. No logic changes. cargo test -p anvilml-scheduler --features mock-hardware exits 0.

description: anvilml-scheduler: retrofit mandatory job-store and queue DEBUG log points (job_store.rs, queue.rs)
context: In job_store.rs: add tracing::debug!(job_id = %job.id, "job inserted into DB") at the end of insert_job(); add tracing::debug!(job_id = %id, status = ?status, "job status updated in DB") at the end of update_status(). In queue.rs: add tracing::debug!(job_id = %job.id, queue_len = self.len(), "job enqueued") at the end of enqueue(); add tracing::debug!(job_id = %job.id, "job dequeued") when pop_next() returns Some. These are §11.5 mandatory DEBUG points. tracing is already a dependency of anvilml-scheduler. No logic changes. cargo test -p anvilml-scheduler --features mock-hardware exits 0.

No source changes. Run and record verbatim output: (1) cargo clippy --workspace --features mock-hardware -- -D warnings; (2) env -i HOME=$HOME PATH=$PATH ANVILML_WORKER_MOCK=1 ANVILML_VENV_PATH=./worker/.venv cargo test --workspace --features mock-hardware; (3) cargo check --workspace --features mock-hardware --target x86_64-pc-windows-gnu; (4) python -m pytest worker/tests/ -v. All four must exit 0. Write the verbatim outputs as the implementation report body. This task is COMPLETE only when all four exit 0 with zero warnings and zero test failures.
